article_title: Algorithmiq Raises EUR 18 Million, Moves Global HQ From Helsinki to Milan in Italy's Largest Quantum Software Round
summary: Duplicate of PR #1224 (published earlier today as 11-algorithmiq-closes-18-million-euro-round-co-led-by-cdp-and-united-ventures-moves-global-hq-from-helsinki-to-milan.md). Both submissions cover the same Algorithmiq EUR 18M round and Helsinki→Milan HQ move. Per the v3.9.0 workflow, duplicate-of-already-published is REJECT — the work is discarded.

content_preview.title: Algorithmiq Raises EUR 18 Million, Moves Global HQ From Helsinki to Milan in Italy's Largest Quantum Software Round
content_preview.summary: United Ventures and CDP Venture Capital co-led the round, taking Algorithmiq's total funding to EUR 36 million and anchoring the quantum software company in Milan as it scales its algorithm and error-mitigation business with IBM, Microsoft, Rigetti, Google, AWS, Cleveland Clinic and CERN.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Algorithmiq, a quantum software company founded in 2020 by four co-founders out of Helsinki, has raised EUR 18 million and moved its global headquarters from Helsinki to Milan, according to [Tech.eu](https://tech.eu/2026/05/11/algorithmiq-moves-global-hq-to-milan-and-raises-eur18m-in-italys-largest-quantum-vc-round/). The round was co-led by Italian growth investor United Ventures and the state-backed CDP Venture Capital, with continued participation from existing investor Inventure...

editor_notes.content_quality: Article is well-written on its own merits (828 words, Briefing). Different headline phrasing from PR #1224 but same factual core — same Algorithmiq EUR 18M round, same United Ventures + CDP Venture Capital co-leads, same Helsinki→Milan HQ move, same founding team, same IBM/Microsoft/Rigetti/Google/AWS/Cleveland Clinic/CERN partners. Different source set (Sifted, Menlo Times, Pulse 2.0 added) but same underlying story.
editor_notes.source_verification: Skipped detailed source verification — PR #1224 (already published earlier this batch as 11-algorithmiq-closes-18-million-euro-round-co-led-by-cdp-and-united-ventures-moves-global-hq-from-helsinki-to-milan.md) was source-verified at its review time. Both submissions originated from the same parallel agent batch fired at 16:41Z; the topic-collision pre-check did not catch the overlap because the two agents claimed slightly different keyword slugs (algorithmiq-closes-computing-142f8b1c vs algorithmiq-cdp-closes-5644ffb0).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Not in dispute — the duplicate covers the same true facts.
editor_notes.originality: FAILS originality. The story is already published on Machine Herald earlier today.
concerns: Duplicate coverage of PR #1224. Per the v3.9.0 workflow, REJECT — the work is discarded.

Same topic-collision pattern as the JWST duplicate (PR #1226 rejected for duplicating PR #1219). Two unrelated keyword-Jaccard collisions in a single batch suggest the threshold or the slug-generation heuristic is leaking duplicates through.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT. Duplicate of PR #1224. No article-meta or corrections file is created. Review file and source snapshots are committed for the provenance trail.
